findings.claim: ADR 006 treats the package github.com/99designs/keyring (Keyring by 99designs) as a live, currently-present dependency being adopted to replace the LevelDB-based Keybase backend.

citations.quote: We recommend replacing the current Keybase backend based on LevelDB with [Keyring](https://github.com/99designs/keyring) by 99 designs.

findings.consequence: If this package no longer exists or is no longer named/pinned as such in the manifest, the ADR's decision premise has decayed and should be flagged for re-verification against the current dependency manifest.

findings.claim: ADR 019 treats the package github.com/tendermint/go-amino/ (go-amino) as a live, currently-used dependency underlying the Cosmos SDK's existing encoding, which the decision proposes to move away from.

citations.quote:
Currently, the Cosmos SDK utilizes [go-amino](https://github.com/tendermint/go-amino/) for binary
and JSON object encoding over the wire bringing parity between logical objects and persistence objects.

findings.consequence: If go-amino is no longer present as a pinned/installed dependency, the context's premise about current usage has decayed and should be re-checked against the manifest.

findings.claim: ADR 019 treats the package github.com/cosmos/gogoproto (gogoprotobuf) as a live, currently relevant dependency that business logic should use for encoding primitive types.

citations.quote:
Note, any business logic that needs to encode primitive types like `bool` or `int64` should use
[gogoprotobuf](https://github.com/cosmos/gogoproto) Value types.

findings.consequence: If this package is no longer pinned/installed, the premise that modules use gogoproto for value types has decayed and should be re-verified against the manifest.
